name: Rushayd al-Hajari
title: Trusted Companion
shortDescription: A special companion of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) whose limbs and tongue were cut by Ibn Ziyad before Ashura, martyring him for his love of Ali (AS).
fullStory:
Rushayd al-Hajari was one of the special, trusted, and very close companions of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS). Imam Ali (AS) had taught him the knowledge of 'Manaya wa Balaya' (awareness of destinies), and his position in Kufa was like that of Maytham al-Tammar.

When Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad entered Kufa to suppress Muslim ibn Aqeel's uprising and create terror, he decided to eliminate prominent Shia figures. He arrested Rushayd al-Hajari and demanded he renounce Ali (AS).

Rushayd bravely refused. Ibn Ziyad ordered his hands and feet to be cut off. With that bloody body, Rushayd shouted the virtues of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) to the people in the Kufa market.

When the news reached Ibn Ziyad, he became furious and ordered his tongue to be cut out as well. Shortly before Ashura, Rushayd al-Hajari was martyred with a chopped body in the path of love for the Ahl al-Bayt, becoming a model of perseverance for true Kufans.
quoteArabic: هَيْهاتَ! وَاللَّهِ لا أَتَبَرَّأُ مِنْ أَمِيرِ المُؤْمِنِينَ...
quote: (In response to Ibn Ziyad's demand to renounce Ali:) Never! By God, I will never renounce Amir al-Mu'minin...
